  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="24579982" data-next-lecture-id="27576694" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/24579982" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/27576694" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/39428003" data-previous-lecture-id="39428003">

    7. Master Your Book Ends
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=3 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=44218991, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-44218991">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p>Time management is your biggest challenge.</p><p>But I don't blame you.</p><p>I blame society.</p><p>Because society has labeled artists with a certain type of behavior... And artists bought into it.</p><p>You're often late... You wait for inspiration to strike... You're moodier than 9 to 5ers... You're an over-thinker... You're a loner...</p><p>You do all the things that society thinks constitute "artist behavior".</p><p>"Artist behavior" may help you get an emo girlfriend or boyfriend... But it won't help you be creative.</p><p>If you want to maximize your potential and achieve "flow", you need to stop seeing yourself how society sees you. </p><p>Don't let them brand you an "artist". (Especially when the normals are using "artist" as a dismissal.)</p><p><span style="background-color: var(--clinical);">When you're in creation mode, you need to see yourself as </span>an athlete.</p><p>Watch any of those Nike ads with sports figures. They could work just as well with a photographer, a writer, a painter, a designer, a hairdresser, or a musician...</p><p>Being creative requires just as much discipline as sports does. Expecially when you're on the path to excellence.</p><p>It requires FOCUS to become the G.O.A.T at anything.</p><p>Every day for us on this journey is "do or die".</p><p>But you'll need to master your TIME if you want to achieve this high level of athletic discipline.</p><p>And this is only possible when you...</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>Master Your BOOK-ENDS.</strong></h1><p>What you do in the morning.</p><p>What you do at night.</p><p>Always start here.</p><p>Especially if you sacrifice large chunks of time to a day job...</p><p>Especially if you aren't ready for a full-on God Momentum yet...</p><p>Master your book-ends.</p><p>Your mornings and your evenings.</p><p>That way anything that happens in-between can never bring you down.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>MORNINGS.</strong></h1><p><em>First, let's talk about the </em><strong><em>second most (yes, second) </em></strong><em>crucial part of your day:</em></p><p>Every athlete has A DAILY PRACTICE.</p><p>A DAILY PRACTICE is what keeps your subconscious in the zone even when you're doing life's daily chores later... Or even if you have to go to a shitty day job afterward... Or when your out-of-town family takes over your apartment for the weekend.</p><p>To develop a morning practice:</p><p>Pick ONE thing that you can do every morning WITHOUT FAIL. </p><p>Something that will elevate your creative game.</p><p>Something where you can see progress.</p><p>Examples:</p><p>• Learn a new technique for your craft and practice applying it for 30 minutes.</p><p>• Practice an instrument</p><p>• Try to recreate photographs you love for an hour</p><p>• Write 'morning pages' (continuous writing without editing for 30 minutes straight) then edit what yesterday's morning pages for 10 minutes</p><p>You get it.</p><p>Wake up an hour earlier if you have to.</p><p><strong>But always, always, always do your DAILY PRACTICE in the morning. (When I was on tour, even though I was doing creative work that I loved, I still woke up to improve my skills-- FOR MYSELF)</strong></p><p>This will put your brain in creativity mode.</p><p>You will "see" things "different" all day.</p><p>If you have a day job, this simple daily action will make it impossible for "the man" to steal your soul.</p><p>When you go for your lunch break, do something for 15 minutes related to your creative goals. It can be as simple as continuing to read a biography about an artist you love. Or take pictures intensely for a few minutes with a point and shoot or your phone.</p><p>This constant injection of creativity moments in your day will prime you for when you DO get the time to be fully creative later that day. Or on the weekend.</p><p>Now it won't feel like you're starting from scratch every time you sit to create. </p><p>You will ease into the creative process immediately because in essence, you never left. </p><p><em>Compare this to what happens now:</em></p><p>You're exhausted by sun down... You have to try to rev up your energy, your motivation, or your inspiration to do the things you supposedly love!</p><p>Don't beat yourself up. It's not your fault... It's because you haven't mastered your book-ends. Starting with your DAILY PRACTICE.</p><p>Having A DAILY PRACTICE is what keeps you in the zone.</p><p>It keeps you connected to your art, your eye, your success.</p><p>Your morning DAILY PRACTICE can be 30 minutes of... making art.</p><p>It can be 30 minutes of... editing art.</p><p>It can be 30 minutes of... promoting your art.</p><p>But whatever you do... Treat it with the same discipline as an athlete.</p><p><em>Which brings us to </em><strong><em>the most essential part of your day</em></strong><em> that you must learn to worship:</em></p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>EVENINGS.</strong></h1><p>People think that a successful day starts in the morning. </p><p>They'll say things like, "I woke up on the wrong side of the bed."</p><p>Or they'll tell you how they need "their morning coffee" to be human.</p><p>Honestly, the morning means nothing if you don't master this first:</p><p><strong>Plan your day-- the night before.</strong></p><p>You can predict how successful your week will be by seeing what you planned (or didn't plan) the night before. </p><p><em>If you don't plan what tomorrow will look like... You're only going to reach a fraction of your potential.</em></p><p>And worst of all: other people will move you around like a pawn on a chessboard.</p><p>Your "priorities" will float away like leaves in the wind.</p><p>You're reading 'The Book Of Money' now. That's no longer acceptable.</p><p><strong>"Plan your day-- the night before."</strong></p><p>It only takes 10 minutes— AKA 1% of your day— to do it.</p><p>You spend more time than that on Instagram, eagerly rotting your brain.</p><p>This action is just as easy to do... Except this action will turn your whole life around:</p><p><strong>"Plan your day--- the night before."</strong></p><p>Choose something you use in your DAILY PRACTICE (paintbrush, instrument, note pad) and place it next to you on the nightstand... Ready for action. </p><p>Make it the first thing you see when you wake up.</p><p>Make it the first thing you reach for when you wake up. Not your phone.</p><p>Put down ON PAPER the steps you will be taking tomorrow to advance your art. </p><p>When you make these thoughts your last ones of the day... You're setting yourself up for success. </p><p>You are priming yourself.</p><p>Your subconscious will start preparing as you sleep.</p><p>No one talks about this because it's not 'sexy'.</p><p>In most "success circles", people only talk about their daily routine. They fetishize: "What time do you wake up? etc..."</p><p>When the sauce is really, "What is your nightly routine?" "How much sleep do you get?"</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>SLEEP.</strong></h1><p>Your ego will want to get the better of you.</p><p>You'll want to walk around and say "I only need 4 hours of sleep," like you're Gary V.</p><p>How many athletes do you know that brag about not needing sleep?</p><p>Exactly.</p><p>And you're a creative athlete, remember? You need sleep.</p><p>Stop acting like "an artist" with all those "artists ups and downs". And all that "artist overthinking".</p><p><em>You're a high-output creative professional. </em></p><p>If the new label doesn't feel comfortable yet, it will start to... After you master your book-ends.</p><p>That way, everything that happens in-between can only affect you on that one particular day...</p><p><em>But it will never have any impact on your self-esteem.</em></p><p></p>
</div>

<p></p><h1><strong>ACTION TIME</strong></h1><p>Reading is nice. I hope you enjoyed that.</p><p><em>But your life won't change without action.</em></p><p><strong>Don't let this be another thing that you read but fail to apply.</strong></p><p>Here are the action steps to take for this module:</p><p><br class="softbreak" style="display: none;"></p><h2>1. WRITE DOWN ONE THING YOU CAN DO EVERY SINGLE DAY WITHOUT FAIL. SOMETHING WHERE YOU CAN SEE PROGRESS / IMPROVEMENT OVER TIME. IT CAN BE SOMETHING THAT TAKES AS LITTLE AS 10-30 MINUTES. WHAT MATTERS IS THAT YOU PICK SOMETHING THAT YOU NEVER HAVE TO MISS A DAY WITH. WHAT IS THE ONE THING THAT IF YOU GOT IT DONE WOULD MAKE THE DAY A GREAT DAY? YOUR DAILY PRACTICE CAN BE AN 30 MINUTES OF... MAKING ART. IT CAN BE 30 MINUTES OF... EDITING ART. IT CAN BE 30 MINUTES OF... PROMOTING YOUR ART.</h2><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h2>2. TONIGHT... SCHEDULE YOUR DAY TOMORROW. PREPARE EVERYTHING RELATED TO YOUR DAILY PRACTICE SO IT'S WAITING FOR YOU WHEN YOU WAKE UP. TAKE OUT YOUR TOOLS. PUT OUT YOUR CLOTHES. GET YOUR WATER READY. RECHARGE BATTERIES. AND FUCK NO: YOU'RE NOT ALLOWED TO TOUCH YOUR PHONE UNTIL AFTER YOU'VE NURTURED YOUR SOUL. PROGRAM YOUR MIND WITH YOUR CREATIVITY AND FURTHERING YOUR GOALS-- NOT WITH WHAT INSTAGRAM'S ALGORITHM DECIDES TO DISTRACT YOU WITH ON THE FEED.</h2><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h2>3. TONIGHT (CONTINUED)... WRITE DOWN WHAT YOU WILL DO TOMORROW <em>AFTER </em>WORK. PREPARE YOUR ENVIRONMENT SO THAT EVERYTHING RELATED TO YOUR "AFTER WORK ACTIVITIES" ARE WAITING READY FOR YOU WHEN YOU WALK THROUGH THOSE DOORS. ALSO PLAN TO CREATE YOUR WRITTEN PLAN FOR THE FOLLOWING DAY, JUST LIKE YOU'RE DOING NOW. MAKING TOMORROW'S PLAN IS THE MOST IMPORTANT PART OF YOUR DAY.</h2><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h2>4. BE RELIGIOUS ABOUT YOUR BOOK ENDS AND YOUR LIFE WILL SPIRAL UPWARDS.</h2><p></p>
